title: Notion integration tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Notion integration token detected in source code (secret_ prefix). Integration tokens provide full access to Notion workspaces, pages, databases, and content. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to read sensitive documents, modify workspace data, delete pages, access private information, and compromise your entire Notion workspace. Store tokens securely in environment variables and rotate immediately if exposed.

Notion integration token detected in Authorization header. Bearer tokens in HTTP headers provide authenticated API access to Notion workspaces. Exposing these tokens enables attackers to impersonate integrations, access confidential workspace data, manipulate databases and pages, exfiltrate sensitive information, and potentially compromise connected systems. Use secure credential management and environment variables.

Notion integration token variable detected in configuration. Hardcoded Notion tokens in source code provide direct workspace access credentials. This exposure allows unauthorized users to read workspace content, modify databases, delete pages, access private information, and potentially leak confidential business data. Always use environment variables or secure secret management systems.

Notion OAuth access token detected (ntat_ prefix). OAuth tokens provide user-authorized access to Notion workspaces. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to act as the authorized user, access personal workspaces, read private pages, modify user content, and potentially compromise linked accounts. Tokens should be stored securely and rotated if compromised.
